library(tidyverse)
library(car)
library(dplyr)
library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(zoo) # na.locf

library(janitor)
library(skimr)

library(tableone)

library(tableone)
library(gtsummary)

1 Import and Cleaning

METABRIC_RNA_Mutation <- read.csv("METABRIC_RNA_Mutation.csv")
dat0 <- METABRIC_RNA_Mutation %>%
  as_tibble()

# rename variables
dat1 <- dat0 %>%
  transmute(
    patient_id,
    er_status,
    hormone_therapy,
    chemotherapy,
    os_months = overall_survival_months,
    os_event = overall_survival,
    
    age = age_at_diagnosis,
    tumor_size,
    nodes_positive = lymph_nodes_examined_positive,
    grade = neoplasm_histologic_grade,
    her2 = her2_status,
    pam50 = `pam50_._claudin.low_subtype`,
    tumor_stage,
    menopausal_state = inferred_menopausal_state,
    pr_status,
    surgery = type_of_breast_surgery,
    radio_therapy
  )

# cleaning variables
dat2 <- dat1 %>%
  mutate(
    across(where(is.character), ~na_if(.x, "")),
    across(c(er_status, hormone_therapy, chemotherapy,
             grade, her2, pam50, tumor_stage,
             menopausal_state, pr_status, surgery, radio_therapy), as.character),
    across(c(age, tumor_size, nodes_positive, os_months, os_event), as.numeric)
  )
table(dat2$er_status, useNA = "ifany")

BREAST CONSERVING        MASTECTOMY              <NA> 
              755              1127                22 
table(dat2$radio_therapy, useNA = "ifany")

   0    1 
 767 1137 
dat_table1 <- dat2 %>%
  filter(
    er_status == "Positive",
    hormone_therapy == 1
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    chemo = chemotherapy,
    trt_group = factor(
      chemo,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("Hormone only", "Hormone + Chemo")
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(chemo),
    !is.na(os_months),
    !is.na(os_event)
  )

nrow(dat0)
[1] 1904
nrow(dat_table1)
[1] 1047
table(dat_table1$trt_group, useNA = "ifany")

   Hormone only Hormone + Chemo 
            924             123 
prop.table(table(dat_table1$trt_group))

   Hormone only Hormone + Chemo 
      0.8825215       0.1174785 
vars_check <- c(
  "age", "tumor_size", "nodes_positive",
  "grade", "her2", "pam50", "tumor_stage",
  "menopausal_state", "pr_status",
  "surgery", "radio_therapy"
)

missing_by_group_long <- dat_table1 %>%
  group_by(trt_group) %>%
  summarise(
    across(all_of(vars_check), ~mean(is.na(.))*100)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    -trt_group,
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "pct_missing"
  )

missing_by_group_long

2 Table 1: covariate distributions over Trt Groups

# raw version
vars <- c(
  "age", "tumor_size", "nodes_positive",
  "grade", "her2", "pam50", "tumor_stage",
  "menopausal_state", "pr_status", "surgery", "radio_therapy"
)

factorVars <- c(
  "grade", "her2", "pam50", "tumor_stage",
  "menopausal_state", "pr_status", "surgery", "radio_therapy"
)

tab1 <- CreateTableOne(
  vars = vars,
  strata = "trt_group",
  data = dat_table1,
  factorVars = factorVars,
  includeNA = TRUE
)

print(
  tab1,
  showAllLevels = TRUE,
  quote = FALSE,
  noSpaces = TRUE,
  nonnormal = c("tumor_size", "nodes_positive")
)

                                101 (82.1)
2.0.0.1 numeric table

Patients receiving chemotherapy were significantly younger (mean age 53.6 vs 66.1 years, p < 0.001) and had more aggressive tumor features, including larger tumor size, greater lymph node involvement, and higher histologic grade (all p < 0.001). Pre-menopausal status was also more common among patients receiving chemotherapy (38.2% vs 8.0%, p < 0.001). HER2 status and PR status did not significantly differ between treatment groups.

These findings suggest that treatment assignment was strongly associated with baseline risk factors.

summary_table <- function(data, vars, group_var = "trt_group") {
  
  out <- lapply(vars, function(v) {
    x <- data[[v]]
    g <- data[[group_var]]
    
    g1 <- as.numeric(x[g == "Hormone only"])
    g2 <- as.numeric(x[g == "Hormone + Chemo"])
    
    ttest <- t.test(g1, g2)
    
    mean1 <- mean(g1, na.rm = TRUE)
    sd1 <- sd(g1, na.rm = TRUE)
    mean2 <- mean(g2, na.rm = TRUE)
    sd2 <- sd(g2, na.rm = TRUE)
    smd <- (mean2 - mean1) / sqrt((sd1^2 + sd2^2) / 2)
    
    data.frame(
      Variable = v,
      Mean_Group1 = mean1,
      SD_Group1 = sd1,
      Mean_Group2 = mean2,
      SD_Group2 = sd2,
      t_value = as.numeric(ttest$statistic),
      p_value = ttest$p.value,
      SMD = smd
    )
  })
  
  dplyr::bind_rows(out)
}

cont_vars <- c("age", "tumor_size", "nodes_positive")

cont_table <- summary_table(dat_table1, cont_vars) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 3))
  )

print(cont_table)
        Variable Mean_Group1 SD_Group1 Mean_Group2 SD_Group2 t_value p_value
1            age      66.059    10.903      53.567    10.719  12.118   0.000
2     tumor_size      25.979    12.859      32.039    19.684  -3.308   0.001
3 nodes_positive       1.979     3.693       4.707     6.180  -4.783   0.000
     SMD
1 -1.156
2  0.365
3  0.536
2.0.0.2 categorical table
categorical_table <- function(data, vars, group_var = "trt_group") {
  
  out <- lapply(vars, function(v) {
    x <- data[[v]]
    g <- data[[group_var]]
    
    # convert to character and keep missing as a level
    x_chr <- as.character(x)
    x_chr[is.na(x_chr)] <- "Missing"
    
    g_chr <- as.character(g)
    
    tab <- table(x_chr, g_chr)
    prop <- prop.table(tab, margin = 2) * 100
    
    # choose test automatically
    test <- if (any(tab < 5)) fisher.test(tab) else chisq.test(tab)
    
    stat_value <- if ("statistic" %in% names(test)) as.numeric(test$statistic) else NA_real_
    p_value <- test$p.value
    
    # avoid errors from missing group columns
    n1 <- if ("Hormone only" %in% colnames(tab)) tab[, "Hormone only"] else rep(0, nrow(tab))
    n2 <- if ("Hormone + Chemo" %in% colnames(tab)) tab[, "Hormone + Chemo"] else rep(0, nrow(tab))
    
    p1 <- if ("Hormone only" %in% colnames(prop)) prop[, "Hormone only"] else rep(0, nrow(prop))
    p2 <- if ("Hormone + Chemo" %in% colnames(prop)) prop[, "Hormone + Chemo"] else rep(0, nrow(prop))
    
    df <- data.frame(
      Variable = v,
      Level = rownames(tab),
      n_Group1 = as.numeric(n1),
      pct_Group1 = as.numeric(p1),
      n_Group2 = as.numeric(n2),
      pct_Group2 = as.numeric(p2),
      statistic = NA_real_,
      p_value = NA_real_,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    
    # put test results on the first row only
    df$statistic[1] <- stat_value
    df$p_value[1] <- p_value
    
    df
  })
  
  dplyr::bind_rows(out)
}

cat_vars <- c(
  "grade",
  "her2",
  "pam50",
  "tumor_stage",
  "menopausal_state",
  "pr_status",
  "surgery",
  "radio_therapy"
)

cat_table <- categorical_table(dat_table1, cat_vars) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    dplyr::across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 3))
  )

2.1 RMST

ps_vars <- c(
  "chemo",
  "trt_group",
  "os_months",
  "os_event",
  "age",
  "tumor_size",
  "nodes_positive",
  "grade",
  "pam50"
)

dat_km <- dat_table1 %>%
  select(all_of(ps_vars)) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(chemo),
    !is.na(os_months),
    !is.na(os_event),
    !is.na(age),
    !is.na(tumor_size),
    !is.na(nodes_positive),
    !is.na(grade),
    !is.na(pam50)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    chemo = as.numeric(chemo)
  )
library(survRM2)
Warning: package 'survRM2' was built under R version 4.4.3
rmst_res <- rmst2(
  time   = dat_km$os_months,
  status = dat_km$os_event,
  arm    = dat_km$chemo,
  tau    = 100
)
rmst_res

The truncation time: tau = 100  was specified. 

Restricted Mean Survival Time (RMST) by arm 
               Est.    se lower .95 upper .95
RMST (arm=1) 95.226 1.276    92.725    97.726
RMST (arm=0) 95.518 0.517    94.505    96.532


Restricted Mean Time Lost (RMTL) by arm 
              Est.    se lower .95 upper .95
RMTL (arm=1) 4.774 1.276     2.274     7.275
RMTL (arm=0) 4.482 0.517     3.468     5.495


Between-group contrast 
                       Est. lower .95 upper .95     p
RMST (arm=1)-(arm=0) -0.293    -2.991     2.406 0.832
RMST (arm=1)/(arm=0)  0.997     0.969     1.026 0.832
RMTL (arm=1)/(arm=0)  1.065     0.602     1.885 0.828

3 Crude KM & Log-rank test: unadjusted association

3.1 KM curve

The Kaplan–Meier estimator was used to estimate the survival function for each treatment group. The log-rank test was used to compare the survival distributions between treatment groups.

library(survival)
library(survminer)
ps_vars <- c(
  "chemo",
  "trt_group",
  "os_months",
  "os_event",
  "age",
  "tumor_size",
  "nodes_positive",
  "grade",
  "pam50"
)

dat_km <- dat_table1 %>%
  select(all_of(ps_vars)) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(chemo),
    !is.na(os_months),
    !is.na(os_event),
    !is.na(age),
    !is.na(tumor_size),
    !is.na(nodes_positive),
    !is.na(grade),
    !is.na(pam50)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    chemo = as.numeric(chemo)
  )
  


table(dat_km$trt_group)

   Hormone only Hormone + Chemo 
            880             120 
prop.table(table(dat_km$trt_group))

   Hormone only Hormone + Chemo 
           0.88            0.12 
tau <- 100

fit_km <- survfit(
  Surv(os_months, os_event) ~ trt_group,
  data = dat_km
)

ggsurvplot(
  fit_km,
  data = dat_km,
  risk.table = TRUE,
  pval = TRUE,
  conf.int = TRUE,
  xlab = "Time since diagnosis (months)",
  ylab = "Overall survival probability",
  legend.title = "Treatment group",
  legend.labs = c("Hormone only", "Hormone + Chemo"),
  xlim = c(0, tau),
  break.time.by = 25,
  ggtheme = theme_bw()
)
summary(fit_km, times = tau)
Call: survfit(formula = Surv(os_months, os_event) ~ trt_group, data = dat_km)

                trt_group=Hormone only 
        time       n.risk      n.event     survival      std.err lower 95% CI 
    100.0000     513.0000      94.0000       0.8665       0.0129       0.8416 
upper 95% CI 
      0.8923 

                trt_group=Hormone + Chemo 
        time       n.risk      n.event     survival      std.err lower 95% CI 
    100.0000      61.0000      19.0000       0.7843       0.0443       0.7021 
upper 95% CI 
      0.8761

The crude Kaplan-Meier curves suggested worse survival in the chemotherapy group. However, this comparison is unadjusted and may reflect baseline differences in disease severity rather than treatment effect.

survdiff(Surv(os_months, os_event) ~ trt_group, data = dat_km)
Call:
survdiff(formula = Surv(os_months, os_event) ~ trt_group, data = dat_km)

                            N Observed Expected (O-E)^2/E (O-E)^2/V
trt_group=Hormone only    880      354    392.8      3.82        57
trt_group=Hormone + Chemo 120       68     29.2     51.37        57

 Chisq= 57  on 1 degrees of freedom, p= 4e-14

4 Weighted KM and Log-rank Test

4.1 calculate propensity score

set up dataset for Propensity score models:

ps_vars <- c(
  "chemo",
  "trt_group",
  "os_months",
  "os_event",
  "age",
  "tumor_size",
  "nodes_positive",
  "grade",
  "pam50"
)

dat_ps <- dat_table1 %>%
  select(all_of(ps_vars)) %>%
  mutate(
    pam50_grp = case_when(
      pam50 %in% c("LumA", "LumB") ~ pam50,
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(chemo),
    !is.na(os_months),
    !is.na(os_event),
    !is.na(age),
    !is.na(tumor_size),
    !is.na(nodes_positive),
    !is.na(grade),
    !is.na(pam50_grp)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    chemo = as.numeric(chemo),
    grade = factor(grade),
    pam50_grp = factor(pam50_grp, levels = c("LumA", "LumB", "Other"))
  )
  

nrow(dat_table1)
[1] 1047
nrow(dat_ps)
[1] 1000
table(dat_ps$trt_group)

   Hormone only Hormone + Chemo 
            880             120 
prop.table(table(dat_ps$trt_group))

   Hormone only Hormone + Chemo 
           0.88            0.12

4.2 fit propensity score model and calculate IPTW

A logistic regression model was used to estimate the propensity score for receiving chemotherapy.

ps_fit <- glm(
  chemo ~ age + tumor_size + nodes_positive + grade + pam50_grp,
  data = dat_ps,
  family = binomial()
)

summary(ps_fit)

Call:
glm(formula = chemo ~ age + tumor_size + nodes_positive + grade + 
    pam50_grp, family = binomial(), data = dat_ps)

Coefficients:
                Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
(Intercept)     2.689081   0.745980   3.605 0.000312 ***
age            -0.107466   0.010868  -9.889  < 2e-16 ***
tumor_size      0.024736   0.007091   3.488 0.000486 ***
nodes_positive  0.113761   0.022529   5.050 4.43e-07 ***
grade2          0.510368   0.493025   1.035 0.300587    
grade3          1.125093   0.489501   2.298 0.021536 *  
pam50_grpLumB  -0.319817   0.274153  -1.167 0.243385    
pam50_grpOther  0.086350   0.280779   0.308 0.758435    
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

(Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)

    Null deviance: 733.85  on 999  degrees of freedom
Residual deviance: 544.77  on 992  degrees of freedom
AIC: 560.77

Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 6

calculate stabilized IPTW:

dat_ps <- dat_ps %>%
  mutate(
    ps = predict(ps_fit, type = "response"),
    p_treat = mean(chemo == 1),
    sw = if_else(
      chemo == 1,
      p_treat / ps,
      (1 - p_treat) / (1 - ps)
    ),
    iptw = if_else(
      chemo == 1,
      1 / ps,
      1 / (1 - ps)
    )
  )

4.3 check overlap

dat_ps %>%
  group_by(trt_group) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    min_ps = min(ps),
    q1_ps = quantile(ps, 0.25),
    median_ps = median(ps),
    mean_ps = mean(ps),
    q3_ps = quantile(ps, 0.75),
    max_ps = max(ps)
  )
# A tibble: 2 × 8
  trt_group           n  min_ps  q1_ps median_ps mean_ps q3_ps max_ps
  <fct>           <int>   <dbl>  <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>
1 Hormone only      880 0.00185 0.0213    0.0485  0.0908 0.108  0.922
2 Hormone + Chemo   120 0.00583 0.135     0.277   0.334  0.468  0.919
ggplot(dat_ps, aes(x = ps, fill = trt_group)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.35) +
  labs(
    x = "Propensity score",
    y = "Density",
    fill = "Treatment group"
  ) +
  theme_bw()
ps_range <- dat_ps %>%
  group_by(trt_group) %>%
  summarise(
    min_ps = min(ps),
    max_ps = max(ps)
  )

ps_range
# A tibble: 2 × 3
  trt_group        min_ps max_ps
  <fct>             <dbl>  <dbl>
1 Hormone only    0.00185  0.922
2 Hormone + Chemo 0.00583  0.919

check weight distribution:

summary(dat_ps$sw)
   Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
 0.1306  0.8954  0.9196  1.0265  0.9851 20.5983 
quantile(dat_ps$sw, probs = c(0, 0.01, 0.05, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.95, 0.99, 1))
        0%         1%         5%        25%        50%        75%        95% 
 0.1305521  0.1516082  0.3270123  0.8954434  0.9196420  0.9850688  1.4409868 
       99%       100% 
 3.0403381 20.5982836 
ggplot(dat_ps, aes(x = sw, fill = trt_group)) +
  geom_histogram(position = "identity", alpha = 0.35, bins = 30) +
  labs(
    x = "Stabilized IPTW",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Treatment group"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

balance before weighting:

vars_balance <- c(
  "age",
  "tumor_size",
  "nodes_positive",
  "grade",
  "pam50_grp"
)

factorVars_balance <- c(
  "grade",
  "pam50_grp"
)

tab_unw <- CreateTableOne(
  vars = vars_balance,
  strata = "trt_group",
  data = dat_ps,
  factorVars = factorVars_balance,
  includeNA = TRUE,
  smd = TRUE
)

print(
  tab_unw,
  showAllLevels = TRUE,
  quote = FALSE,
  noSpaces = TRUE,
  smd = TRUE,
  nonnormal = c("tumor_size", "nodes_positive")
)
                               Stratified by trt_group
                                level Hormone only         Hormone + Chemo     
  n                                   880                  120                 
  age (mean (SD))                     65.87 (10.88)        53.59 (10.82)       
  tumor_size (median [IQR])           23.00 [18.00, 30.00] 26.00 [21.00, 37.50]
  nodes_positive (median [IQR])       1.00 [0.00, 2.00]    2.00 [1.00, 6.00]   
  grade (%)                     1     95 (10.8)            6 (5.0)             
                                2     416 (47.3)           42 (35.0)           
                                3     369 (41.9)           72 (60.0)           
  pam50_grp (%)                 LumA  389 (44.2)           51 (42.5)           
                                LumB  319 (36.2)           36 (30.0)           
                                Other 172 (19.5)           33 (27.5)           
                               Stratified by trt_group
                                p      test    SMD  
  n                                                 
  age (mean (SD))               <0.001         1.132
  tumor_size (median [IQR])     <0.001 nonnorm 0.365
  nodes_positive (median [IQR]) <0.001 nonnorm 0.555
  grade (%)                     0.001          0.385
                                                    
                                                    
  pam50_grp (%)                 0.106          0.199

balance after weighting:

library(survey)
Warning: package 'survey' was built under R version 4.4.3
design_sw <- svydesign(
  ids = ~1,
  weights = ~sw,
  data = dat_ps
)

tab_w <- svyCreateTableOne(
  vars = vars_balance,
  strata = "trt_group",
  data = design_sw,
  factorVars = factorVars_balance,
  includeNA = TRUE,
  smd = TRUE
)

print(
  tab_w,
  showAllLevels = TRUE,
  quote = FALSE,
  noSpaces = TRUE,
  smd = TRUE,
  nonnormal = c("tumor_size", "nodes_positive")
)
                               Stratified by trt_group
                                level Hormone only         Hormone + Chemo     
  n                                   893.08               133.38              
  age (mean (SD))                     64.17 (11.94)        67.90 (13.71)       
  tumor_size (median [IQR])           24.00 [18.00, 30.00] 25.00 [18.00, 26.00]
  nodes_positive (median [IQR])       1.00 [0.00, 2.00]    1.00 [1.00, 2.00]   
  grade (%)                     1     89.7 (10.0)          22.4 (16.8)         
                                2     402.0 (45.0)         55.4 (41.5)         
                                3     401.3 (44.9)         55.6 (41.7)         
  pam50_grp (%)                 LumA  387.9 (43.4)         75.8 (56.8)         
                                LumB  325.3 (36.4)         36.2 (27.2)         
                                Other 179.8 (20.1)         21.4 (16.0)         
                               Stratified by trt_group
                                p      test    SMD  
  n                                                 
  age (mean (SD))               0.278          0.290
  tumor_size (median [IQR])     0.939  nonnorm 0.078
  nodes_positive (median [IQR]) <0.001 nonnorm 0.052
  grade (%)                     0.683          0.198
                                                    
                                                    
  pam50_grp (%)                 0.294          0.271

4.4 compare SMD before and after weighting

SMD < 0.1 → ideal SMD < 0.2 → acceptable SMD > 0.2 → imbalance

smd_unw <- ExtractSmd(tab_unw)
smd_w <- ExtractSmd(tab_w)

smd_compare <- data.frame(
  variable = vars_balance,
  smd_unweighted = as.numeric(smd_unw),
  smd_weighted = as.numeric(smd_w)
) %>%
  arrange(desc(smd_unweighted))

smd_compare
        variable smd_unweighted smd_weighted
1            age      1.1321405   0.28984004
2 nodes_positive      0.5547832   0.05200370
3          grade      0.3853747   0.19827412
4     tumor_size      0.3650600   0.07822265
5      pam50_grp      0.1993822   0.27077692
smd_compare %>%
  mutate(
    imbalance_unweighted = abs(smd_unweighted) > 0.1,
    imbalance_weighted = abs(smd_weighted) > 0.1
  )
        variable smd_unweighted smd_weighted imbalance_unweighted
1            age      1.1321405   0.28984004                 TRUE
2 nodes_positive      0.5547832   0.05200370                 TRUE
3          grade      0.3853747   0.19827412                 TRUE
4     tumor_size      0.3650600   0.07822265                 TRUE
5      pam50_grp      0.1993822   0.27077692                 TRUE
  imbalance_weighted
1               TRUE
2              FALSE
3               TRUE
4              FALSE
5               TRUE

We estimated a propensity score for receiving chemotherapy using baseline clinical covariates age, tumor size, number of positive lymph nodes, histologic grade, and PAM50 subtype, then constructed stabilized inverse probability of treatment weights. The propensity score distributions showed adequate overlap between treatment groups, although the chemotherapy group had generally larger scores, consistent with confounding by indication. The stabilized weights were overall well behaved, with mean close to 1 and only a few large values, suggesting that the weighted analysis should be reasonably stable. Covariate balance improved meaningfully after weighting, especially for nodal involvement and tumor size, while some residual imbalance remained for age, grade, and PAM50. Overall, these diagnostics suggest that IPTW is appropriate as an adjusted survival comparison, while residual confounding should still be acknowledged.

4.5 IPTW weighted KM

# fit weighted km curves
fit_km_w <- survfit(
  Surv(os_months, os_event) ~ trt_group,
  data = dat_ps,
  weights = sw
)

ggsurvplot(
  fit_km_w,
  data = dat_ps,
  conf.int = TRUE,
  xlab = "Time since diagnosis (months)",
  ylab = "Overall survival probability",
  legend.title = "Treatment group",
  legend.labs = c("Hormone only", "Hormone + Chemo"),
  xlim = c(0, tau),
  break.time.by = 25,
  ggtheme = theme_bw()
)
summary(fit_km_w, times = tau)
Call: survfit(formula = Surv(os_months, os_event) ~ trt_group, data = dat_ps, 
    weights = sw)

                trt_group=Hormone only 
        time       n.risk      n.event     survival      std.err lower 95% CI 
    100.0000     522.5511      97.8985       0.8650       0.0148       0.8364 
upper 95% CI 
      0.8946 

                trt_group=Hormone + Chemo 
        time       n.risk      n.event     survival      std.err lower 95% CI 
    100.0000      56.3584       7.2110       0.9015       0.0337       0.8377 
upper 95% CI 
      0.9701 
kmw_150 <- summary(fit_km_w, times = tau)

data.frame(
  trt_group = kmw_150$strata,
  time = kmw_150$time,
  surv = kmw_150$surv,
  lower = kmw_150$lower,
  upper = kmw_150$upper
)
                  trt_group time      surv     lower     upper
1    trt_group=Hormone only  100 0.8650449 0.8364480 0.8946194
2 trt_group=Hormone + Chemo  100 0.9014609 0.8376997 0.9700753

4.6 IPTW weighted logrank

design_sw <- svydesign(
  ids = ~1,
  weights = ~sw,
  data = dat_ps
)
library(survey)

fit_wlr <- svylogrank(
  Surv(os_months, os_event) ~ trt_group,
  design = design_sw
)

fit_wlr
[[1]]
        score                              
[1,] 16.79338 6.125427 2.741585 0.006114355

[[2]]
      Chisq           p 
7.516287863 0.006114355 

attr(,"class")
[1] "svylogrank"

4.7 IPTW weighted cox

dat_wcox <- dat_table1 %>%
  select(
    patient_id,
    chemo,
    trt_group,
    os_months,
    os_event,
    age,
    tumor_size,
    nodes_positive,
    grade,
    pam50
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pam50_grp = case_when(
      pam50 %in% c("LumA", "LumB") ~ pam50,
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(patient_id),
    !is.na(chemo),
    !is.na(os_months),
    !is.na(os_event),
    !is.na(age),
    !is.na(tumor_size),
    !is.na(nodes_positive),
    !is.na(grade),
    !is.na(pam50_grp)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    chemo = as.numeric(chemo),
    grade = factor(grade),
    pam50_grp = factor(pam50_grp, levels = c("LumA", "LumB", "Other"))
  )
ps_fit <- glm(
  chemo ~ age + tumor_size + nodes_positive + grade + pam50_grp,
  data = dat_wcox,
  family = binomial()
)

dat_wcox <- dat_wcox %>%
  mutate(
    ps = predict(ps_fit, type = "response"),
    p_treat = mean(chemo == 1),
    sw = if_else(
      chemo == 1,
      p_treat / ps,
      (1 - p_treat) / (1 - ps)
    )
  )

4.7.1 weighted cox without time-dependent chemotherapy:

cox_w <- coxph(
  Surv(os_months, os_event) ~
    chemo +
    age +
    tumor_size +
    nodes_positive +
    grade +
    pam50_grp,
  data = dat_wcox,
  weights = sw,
  ties = "breslow",
  robust = TRUE,
  id = patient_id
)

summary(cox_w)
Call:
coxph(formula = Surv(os_months, os_event) ~ chemo + age + tumor_size + 
    nodes_positive + grade + pam50_grp, data = dat_wcox, weights = sw, 
    ties = "breslow", robust = TRUE, id = patient_id)

  n= 1000, number of events= 422 

                    coef exp(coef)  se(coef) robust se      z Pr(>|z|)  
chemo           0.558749  1.748484  0.175773  0.217748  2.566   0.0103 *
age            -0.017621  0.982533  0.004692  0.007653 -2.302   0.0213 *
tumor_size     -0.008927  0.991113  0.004355  0.005171 -1.726   0.0843 .
nodes_positive -0.015568  0.984553  0.011852  0.023293 -0.668   0.5039  
grade2         -0.139389  0.869890  0.151194  0.184016 -0.757   0.4488  
grade3         -0.457517  0.632853  0.161070  0.182473 -2.507   0.0122 *
pam50_grpLumB   0.200310  1.221781  0.121678  0.133011  1.506   0.1321  
pam50_grpOther  0.185916  1.204321  0.132536  0.147263  1.262   0.2068  
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

               exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
chemo             1.7485     0.5719    1.1411    2.6792
age               0.9825     1.0178    0.9679    0.9974
tumor_size        0.9911     1.0090    0.9811    1.0012
nodes_positive    0.9846     1.0157    0.9406    1.0305
grade2            0.8699     1.1496    0.6065    1.2477
grade3            0.6329     1.5801    0.4426    0.9049
pam50_grpLumB     1.2218     0.8185    0.9414    1.5857
pam50_grpOther    1.2043     0.8303    0.9024    1.6073

Concordance= 0.592  (se = 0.02 )
Likelihood ratio test= 44.79  on 8 df,   p=4e-07
Wald test            = 40.8  on 8 df,   p=2e-06
Score (logrank) test = 46.52  on 8 df,   p=2e-07,   Robust = 31.27  p=1e-04

  (Note: the likelihood ratio and score tests assume independence of
     observations within a cluster, the Wald and robust score tests do not).
ph_test_w <- cox.zph(cox_w)
ph_test_w
                chisq df       p
chemo          36.132  1 1.8e-09
age             0.187  1    0.67
tumor_size      0.619  1    0.43
nodes_positive  2.058  1    0.15
grade           1.519  2    0.47
pam50_grp       0.431  2    0.81
GLOBAL         42.273  8 1.2e-06
event.times <- sort(unique(dat_wcox$os_months[dat_wcox$os_event == 1]))
event.times <- event.times[-1]

dat_split_w <- survSplit(
  data = dat_wcox,
  cut = event.times,
  end = "os_months",
  start = "t0",
  event = "os_event"
)
dat_split_w <- dat_split_w %>%
  mutate(
    chemo_t = chemo * log(os_months)
  )

4.7.2 weighted cox with time-dependent chemotherapy

cox_w_td <- coxph(
  Surv(t0, os_months, os_event) ~
    chemo +
    chemo_t +
    age +
    tumor_size +
    nodes_positive +
    grade +
    pam50_grp,
  data = dat_split_w,
  weights = sw,
  ties = "breslow",
  robust = TRUE,
  id = patient_id
)

summary(cox_w_td)
Call:
coxph(formula = Surv(t0, os_months, os_event) ~ chemo + chemo_t + 
    age + tumor_size + nodes_positive + grade + pam50_grp, data = dat_split_w, 
    weights = sw, ties = "breslow", robust = TRUE, id = patient_id)

  n= 162869, number of events= 422 

                     coef  exp(coef)   se(coef)  robust se      z Pr(>|z|)

               exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
chemo          1.842e-05  5.429e+04 2.235e-08   0.01517
chemo_t        1.185e+01  8.438e-02 2.992e+00  46.94353
age            9.840e-01  1.016e+00 9.694e-01   0.99881
tumor_size     9.910e-01  1.009e+00 9.812e-01   1.00100

pam50_grpOther 1.203e+00  8.315e-01 9.045e-01   1.59916

Concordance= 0.615  (se = 0.019 )
Likelihood ratio test= 72.21  on 9 df,   p=6e-12
Wald test            = 78.93  on 9 df,   p=3e-13
Score (logrank) test = 64.47  on 9 df,   p=2e-10,   Robust = 36.4  p=3e-05

  (Note: the likelihood ratio and score tests assume independence of
     observations within a cluster, the Wald and robust score tests do not).

4.7.3 clinical-only crude Cox

# set up data for clinical-only crude cox
dat_ccox <- dat_table1 %>%
  select(
    patient_id,
    chemo,
    trt_group,
    os_months,
    os_event,
    age,
    tumor_size,
    nodes_positive,
    grade,
    pam50
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pam50_grp = case_when(
      pam50 %in% c("LumA", "LumB") ~ pam50,
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(patient_id),
    !is.na(chemo),
    !is.na(os_months),
    !is.na(os_event),
    !is.na(age),
    !is.na(tumor_size),
    !is.na(nodes_positive),
    !is.na(grade),
    !is.na(pam50_grp)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    chemo = factor(
      as.numeric(chemo),
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("Hormone only", "Hormone + Chemo")
    ),
    chemo_num = as.numeric(chemo == "Hormone + Chemo"),
    grade = factor(grade),
    pam50_grp = factor(pam50_grp, levels = c("LumA", "LumB", "Other"))
  )

nrow(dat_ccox)
[1] 1000
table(dat_ccox$chemo, useNA = "ifany")

   Hormone only Hormone + Chemo 
            880             120 
table(dat_ccox$grade, useNA = "ifany")

  1   2   3 
101 458 441 
table(dat_ccox$pam50_grp, useNA = "ifany")

 LumA  LumB Other 
  440   355   205 
# fit clinical-only crude cox
ccox_fit <- coxph(
  Surv(os_months, os_event) ~
    chemo +
    age +
    tumor_size +
    nodes_positive +
    grade +
    pam50_grp,
  data = dat_ccox,
  ties = "breslow"
)

summary(ccox_fit)
Call:
coxph(formula = Surv(os_months, os_event) ~ chemo + age + tumor_size + 
    nodes_positive + grade + pam50_grp, data = dat_ccox, ties = "breslow")

  n= 1000, number of events= 422 

                          coef exp(coef)  se(coef)      z Pr(>|z|)

grade2               -0.247451  0.780788  0.148435 -1.667  0.09550 .  
grade3               -0.546556  0.578940  0.157264 -3.475  0.00051 ***
pam50_grpLumB         0.110268  1.116578  0.119943  0.919  0.35792    
pam50_grpOther        0.165268  1.179709  0.128838  1.283  0.19957    
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

                     exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
chemoHormone + Chemo    2.8145     0.3553    2.0691    3.8284
age                     0.9906     1.0095    0.9808    1.0005
tumor_size              0.9901     1.0100    0.9814    0.9988
nodes_positive          0.9796     1.0208    0.9420    1.0186

pam50_grpOther          1.1797     0.8477    0.9164    1.5186

Concordance= 0.608  (se = 0.016 )

Wald test            = 78.32  on 8 df,   p=1e-13
Score (logrank) test = 83.34  on 8 df,   p=1e-14
# check ph assumption
ccox_ph_res <- cox.zph(ccox_fit)
ccox_ph_res
                 chisq df       p
chemo          22.5989  1   2e-06
age             1.9683  1 0.16063
tumor_size      0.6080  1 0.43553
nodes_positive  0.0885  1 0.76606
grade           4.0611  2 0.13126
pam50_grp       2.7652  2 0.25092
GLOBAL         28.0982  8 0.00046
plot(ccox_ph_res)
# set up time-dependent crude cox
ccox_event_times <- sort(unique(dat_ccox$os_months[dat_ccox$os_event == 1]))
ccox_event_times <- ccox_event_times[-1]

dat_ccox_split <- survSplit(
  data = dat_ccox,
  cut = ccox_event_times,
  end = "os_months",
  start = "t0",
  event = "os_event"
) %>%
  mutate(
    chemo_t = chemo_num * log(os_months)
  )
# fit time-dependent crude cox
ccox_td_fit <- coxph(
  Surv(t0, os_months, os_event) ~
    chemo_num +
    chemo_t +
    age +
    tumor_size +
    nodes_positive +
    grade +
    pam50_grp,
  data = dat_ccox_split,
  ties = "breslow"
)

summary(ccox_td_fit)
Call:
coxph(formula = Surv(t0, os_months, os_event) ~ chemo_num + chemo_t + 
    age + tumor_size + nodes_positive + grade + pam50_grp, data = dat_ccox_split, 
    ties = "breslow")

  n= 162869, number of events= 422

ccox_td_ph_res <- cox.zph(ccox_td_fit)
ccox_td_ph_res
               chisq df     p
chemo_num      4.301  1 0.038
chemo_t        4.185  1 0.041
age            0.209  1 0.647
tumor_size     0.131  1 0.718
nodes_positive 0.170  1 0.680
grade          3.146  2 0.207
pam50_grp      3.050  2 0.218
GLOBAL         9.896  9 0.359
plot(ccox_td_ph_res)

5 Gene variable selection: LASSO/SCAD/MCP/coxBAR

The following penalized regression methods were applied for gene selection:

• LASSO (Least Absolute Shrinkage and Selection Operator) • BAR (Broken Adaptive Ridge) • SCAD (Smoothly Clipped Absolute Deviation) • MCP (Minimax Concave Penalty)

library(ncvreg)
Warning: package 'ncvreg' was built under R version 4.4.3

5.1 set up data for penalized regression

The gene data include 331 gene expression z-scores and 175 mutation variables. The expression variables are numeric, whereas the mutation variables are recorded as either 0 or mutation-type strings. Because mutation-type strings are too sparse and high-dimensional for penalized Cox regression, the mutation variables were recoded as binary indicators, with 1 indicating mutation and 0 indicating no mutation.

The analysis was restricted to ER-positive patients who received hormone therapy, consistent with the main study cohort.

We would also restrict the sample to ER+ and hormone_therapy ==1 only, same as our analysis cohort.

dat_gene0 <- dat0 %>%
  select(
    patient_id,
    er_status,
    hormone_therapy,
    chemotherapy,
    os_months = overall_survival_months,
    os_event = overall_survival,
    brca1:siah1_mut
  )

dat_gene1 <- dat_gene0 %>%
  mutate(
    across(where(is.character), ~na_if(.x, "")),
    across(c(er_status, hormone_therapy, chemotherapy), as.character),
    across(c(os_months, os_event), as.numeric),
    across(brca1:ugt2b7, as.numeric),
    across(
      pik3ca_mut:siah1_mut,
      ~case_when(
        is.na(.x) ~ NA_real_,
        .x == "0" ~ 0,
        TRUE ~ 1
      )
    )
  )

dat_lasso0 <- dat_gene1 %>%
  filter(
    er_status == "Positive",
    hormone_therapy == 1
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(chemotherapy),
    !is.na(os_months),
    !is.na(os_event)
  )
gene_df <- dat_lasso0 %>%
  select(brca1:siah1_mut)

col_na <- colSums(is.na(gene_df))

col_var0 <- sapply(gene_df, function(x) var(x, na.rm = TRUE))
table(col_var0 == 0)

FALSE  TRUE 
  660     2 
keep_gene <- names(gene_df)[col_na == 0 & col_var0 > 0]

dat_lasso <- dat_lasso0 %>%
  select(patient_id, er_status, hormone_therapy, chemotherapy, os_months, os_event,
         all_of(keep_gene))
X <- dat_lasso %>%
  select(all_of(keep_gene)) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  as.matrix()

sum(is.na(X)) # should be 0
[1] 0
dim(X) # should be 662-2 = 660
[1] 1047  660
X <- scale(X)

5.2 LASSO

library(ncvreg)

set.seed(215)

fit <- cv.ncvsurv(
  X,
  Surv(dat_lasso$os_months, dat_lasso$os_event),
  penalty = "lasso",
  nlambda = 50,
  nfolds = 10
)
Warning in ncvsurv(X = X, y = y, ...): Algorithm failed to converge for some
values of lambda
fit.lasso <- ncvsurv(
  X,
  Surv(dat_lasso$os_months, dat_lasso$os_event),
  penalty = "lasso",
  lambda = fit$lambda.min
)
Warning: ncvsurv() is intended for pathwise optimization, not for single values of lambda.
  1. You are strongly encouraged to fit a path and extract the solution at the lambda value of interest, rather than use ncvsurv() in this way.
  2. In particular, if you are using the MCP or SCAD penalties, be aware that you greatly increase your risk of converging to an inferior local maximum if you do not fit an entire path.
  3. You may wish to look at the ncvfit() function, which is intended for non-path (i.e., single-lambda) optimization and allows the user to supply initial values.
fit$lambda.min
[1] 0.03630447
range(fit$lambda)
[1] 0.001231891 0.171170744
which.min(abs(fit$lambda - fit$lambda.min))
[1] 12
length(fit$lambda)
[1] 36

selected variables:

lasso_selected <- names(lasso_coef)[lasso_coef != 0]

fit <- cv.ncvsurv(
  X,
  Surv(dat_lasso$os_months, dat_lasso$os_event),
  penalty = "SCAD",
  nlambda = 50,
  nfolds = 10
)

)

)

5.5 coxBAR

library(Cyclops)
Warning: package 'Cyclops' was built under R version 4.4.3
library(BrokenAdaptiveRidge)
Warning: package 'BrokenAdaptiveRidge' was built under R version 4.4.3
library(Cyclops)
library(BrokenAdaptiveRidge)
library(survival)

set.seed(215)

ncov <- ncol(X)
n <- nrow(X)

dataFit <- createCyclopsData(
  Surv(dat_lasso$os_months, dat_lasso$os_event) ~ X,
  modelType = "cox"
)

pen_grid <- exp(seq(log(0.1 * log(ncov)), log(1 * log(ncov)), length.out = 50))

coxbar_grid <- lapply(pen_grid, function(pen) {

  barPrior <- createBarPrior(
    penalty = pen,
    initialRidgeVariance = 1 / pen
  )

  fit.bar <- try(
    fitCyclopsModel(dataFit, prior = barPrior),
    silent = TRUE
  )

  if(inherits(fit.bar, "try-error")) {
    return(list(
      penalty = pen,
      fit = NULL,
      bic = Inf,
      refit_warn = FALSE,
      refit_ok = FALSE
    ))
  }

  coef_bar <- try(coef(fit.bar), silent = TRUE)

  if(inherits(coef_bar, "try-error") || is.null(coef_bar)) {
    return(list(
      penalty = pen,
      fit = NULL,
      bic = Inf,
      refit_warn = FALSE,
      refit_ok = FALSE
    ))
  }

  selected_idx <- which(coef_bar != 0)
  k <- length(selected_idx)

  if(k == 0) {
    fit_null <- coxph(Surv(os_months, os_event) ~ 1, data = dat_lasso)

    bic <- -2 * as.numeric(logLik(fit_null)) + log(n) * k

    return(list(
      penalty = pen,
      fit = fit.bar,
      bic = bic,
      refit_warn = FALSE,
      refit_ok = TRUE
    ))
  } else {

    dat_bar <- data.frame(
      os_months = dat_lasso$os_months,
      os_event = dat_lasso$os_event,
      X[, selected_idx, drop = FALSE]
    )

    colnames(dat_bar)[3:ncol(dat_bar)] <- colnames(X)[selected_idx]

    warn_msg <- NULL

    fit_refit <- tryCatch(
      withCallingHandlers(
        coxph(
          as.formula(
            paste(
              "Surv(os_months, os_event) ~",
              paste(colnames(X)[selected_idx], collapse = " + ")
            )
          ),
          data = dat_bar
        ),
        warning = function(w) {
          warn_msg <<- conditionMessage(w)
          invokeRestart("muffleWarning")
        }
      ),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )

    if(is.null(fit_refit)) {
      bic <- Inf
      refit_ok <- FALSE
    } else {
      bic <- -2 * as.numeric(logLik(fit_refit)) + log(n) * k
      refit_ok <- TRUE
    }

    return(list(
      penalty = pen,
      fit = fit.bar,
      bic = bic,
      refit_warn = !is.null(warn_msg),
      warn_msg = warn_msg,
      refit_ok = refit_ok
    ))
  }
})
bic_values <- sapply(coxbar_grid, function(x) x$bic)

best_index <- which.min(bic_values)

pen_grid[best_index]
[1] 1.313763
range(pen_grid)
[1] 0.649224 6.492240
best_index
[1] 16
length(pen_grid)
[1] 50

best fit model:

fit.coxbar <- coxbar_grid[[best_index]]$fit

coef_bar <- coef(fit.coxbar)

selected variables:

coxbar_selected <- colnames(X)[coef_bar != 0]

coxbar_selected
 [1] "cdkn2b"    "casp8"     "mapk14"    "pdgfa"     "smad4"     "tsc2"     
 [7] "kmt2c"     "bbc3"      "ahnak"     "ldlrap1"   "sbno1"     "setd1a"   
[13] "taf1"      "hsd17b14"  "hsd17b4"   "kmt2d_mut" "ptprd_mut" "nek1_mut" 
coxbar_selected_df <- data.frame(
  variable = colnames(X)[coef_bar != 0],
  coef = coef_bar[coef_bar != 0]
) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(coef)))

coxbar_selected_df
            variable        coef
Xtsc2           tsc2 -0.33246317
Xkmt2c         kmt2c -0.27860009
Xmapk14       mapk14  0.27524096

Xbbc3           bbc3  0.24675829
Xsbno1         sbno1 -0.20964952
Xsetd1a       setd1a  0.18213530
Xldlrap1     ldlrap1  0.17583519
Xtaf1           taf1  0.16373477

Xsmad4         smad4  0.14261635
Xptprd_mut ptprd_mut -0.13929264

Xnek1_mut   nek1_mut -0.12934644

Xkmt2d_mut kmt2d_mut -0.08189969
sum(is.infinite(bic_values))
[1] 2
sum(sapply(coxbar_grid, function(x) x$refit_warn), na.rm = TRUE)
[1] 1
sum(sapply(coxbar_grid, function(x) x$refit_ok), na.rm = TRUE)
[1] 48

Only 2 of the 50 penalty values failed during fitting. Only 1 run produced an infinite-coefficient warning in the refitted Cox model. Overall, 48 penalty values produced stable refitted Cox models.

5.6 compare methods

gene_sets <- list(
  lasso = lasso_selected,
  scad = scad_selected,
  mcp = mcp_selected,
  coxbar = coxbar_selected
)
refit_compare <- lapply(names(gene_sets), function(m) {

  vars <- gene_sets[[m]]

  dat_tmp <- dat_lasso %>%
    select(os_months, os_event, all_of(vars))

  fit_tmp <- coxph(
    as.formula(
      paste("Surv(os_months, os_event) ~", paste(vars, collapse = " + "))
    ),
    data = dat_tmp
  )

  data.frame(
    method = m,
    n_var = length(vars),
    logLik = as.numeric(logLik(fit_tmp)),
    AIC = AIC(fit_tmp),
    BIC = BIC(fit_tmp)
  )
}) %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  arrange(BIC)

refit_compare
  method n_var    logLik      AIC      BIC
1 coxbar    18 -2303.086 4642.172 4715.362
2    mcp    16 -2312.698 4657.397 4722.454
3   scad    51 -2245.468 4592.936 4800.308
4  lasso    44 -2272.039 4632.078 4810.986

5.7 select variables

Our aim was to select 5 to 8 final gene variables for inclusion in the Cox model.

why choose CoxBAR as the main model: In addition that coxBAR gives best BIC, the main reason is because our goal was sparse and stable variable screening in a correlated genomic setting, we treated CoxBAR as the primary penalized Cox selection method. CoxBAR is a surrogate ℓ0 penalized Cox approach based on broken adaptive ridge and has been shown to have selection consistency, oracle property for parameter estimation, and a grouping property for highly correlated covariates. In simulation and empirical evaluations, BAR-based Cox regression selected relatively sparse models and achieved model selection and discriminatory performance comparable to or slightly better than Cox LASSO, with less biased and more stable solution paths under BIC-based tuning.

summarize overlap among selected variables:

all_selected <- list(
  lasso = lasso_selected,
  scad = scad_selected,
  mcp = mcp_selected,
  coxbar = coxbar_selected
)

all_genes <- sort(unique(unlist(all_selected)))

overlap_df <- data.frame(variable = all_genes) %>%
  mutate(
    in_lasso = variable %in% lasso_selected,
    in_scad = variable %in% scad_selected,
    in_mcp = variable %in% mcp_selected,
    in_coxbar = variable %in% coxbar_selected,
    n_methods = in_lasso + in_scad + in_mcp + in_coxbar
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(n_methods), variable)

overlap_df
      variable in_lasso in_scad in_mcp in_coxbar n_methods
1         bbc3     TRUE    TRUE   TRUE      TRUE         4
2     nek1_mut     TRUE    TRUE   TRUE      TRUE         4
3    ptprd_mut     TRUE    TRUE   TRUE      TRUE         4
4        sbno1     TRUE    TRUE   TRUE      TRUE         4
5         tsc2     TRUE    TRUE   TRUE      TRUE         4
6         aff2     TRUE    TRUE   TRUE     FALSE         3
7        apaf1     TRUE    TRUE   TRUE     FALSE         3
8       casp10     TRUE    TRUE   TRUE     FALSE         3
9        casp8     TRUE    TRUE  FALSE      TRUE         3
10  cdkn2a_mut     TRUE    TRUE   TRUE     FALSE         3
11       foxo1     TRUE    TRUE   TRUE     FALSE         3
12     hsd17b4     TRUE    TRUE  FALSE      TRUE         3
13   klrg1_mut     TRUE    TRUE   TRUE     FALSE         3
14       kmt2c     TRUE    TRUE  FALSE      TRUE         3

38       ahnak    FALSE   FALSE  FALSE      TRUE         1
39      ahnak2    FALSE    TRUE  FALSE     FALSE         1
40         atr     TRUE   FALSE  FALSE     FALSE         1
41       ccnb1     TRUE   FALSE  FALSE     FALSE         1
42      cdkn2b    FALSE   FALSE  FALSE      TRUE         1
43       fgfr1    FALSE    TRUE  FALSE     FALSE         1
44       foxp1    FALSE    TRUE  FALSE     FALSE         1
45   gata3_mut    FALSE    TRUE  FALSE     FALSE         1
46    hsd17b11     TRUE   FALSE  FALSE     FALSE         1
47    hsd17b14    FALSE   FALSE  FALSE      TRUE         1
48      hsd3b7     TRUE   FALSE  FALSE     FALSE         1
49        jak2     TRUE   FALSE  FALSE     FALSE         1
50         kit     TRUE   FALSE  FALSE     FALSE         1
51       mmp13    FALSE    TRUE  FALSE     FALSE         1
52       mmp21    FALSE    TRUE  FALSE     FALSE         1
53       muc16    FALSE    TRUE  FALSE     FALSE         1
54         myc     TRUE   FALSE  FALSE     FALSE         1
55   myo3a_mut    FALSE    TRUE  FALSE     FALSE         1
56       pdgfa    FALSE   FALSE  FALSE      TRUE         1
57       prkd1    FALSE    TRUE  FALSE     FALSE         1
58       psen1     TRUE   FALSE  FALSE     FALSE         1
59         rb1     TRUE   FALSE  FALSE     FALSE         1
60      setd1a    FALSE   FALSE  FALSE      TRUE         1
61   setd2_mut    FALSE    TRUE  FALSE     FALSE         1
62       sf3b1     TRUE   FALSE  FALSE     FALSE         1
63       stab2    FALSE    TRUE  FALSE     FALSE         1
64        taf1    FALSE   FALSE  FALSE      TRUE         1
65       taf4b    FALSE    TRUE  FALSE     FALSE         1
66 tbl1xr1_mut    FALSE    TRUE  FALSE     FALSE         1
67        terc     TRUE   FALSE  FALSE     FALSE         1
68  thsd7a_mut    FALSE    TRUE  FALSE     FALSE         1
69   ush2a_mut    FALSE    TRUE  FALSE     FALSE         1

The variables selected by all 4 methods were tsc2, bbc3, sbno1, ptprd_mut, and nek1_mut.

The final selected variables were tsc2, bbc3, sbno1, ptprd_mut, nek1_mut. This set was primarily based on CoxBAR and also showed good stability across selection methods, since the first five variables appeared in all four methods and the remaining two appeared in three methods.

Because ptprd_mut and nek1_mut were originally recorded as either 0 or mutation-type strings, they were recoded as binary mutation indicators, with 1 indicating mutation and 0 indicating no mutation, before being entered into the Cox model.

6 Crude Cox with selected genes

6.1 set up data

hx_dat0 <- METABRIC_RNA_Mutation %>%
  as_tibble()

hx_dat1 <- hx_dat0 %>%
  transmute(
    patient_id,
    er_status,
    hormone_therapy,
    chemotherapy,
    os_months = overall_survival_months,
    os_event  = overall_survival,

    age = age_at_diagnosis,
    tumor_size,
    nodes_positive = lymph_nodes_examined_positive,
    grade = neoplasm_histologic_grade,
    her2 = her2_status,
    pam50 = `pam50_._claudin.low_subtype`,
    tumor_stage,
    menopausal_state = inferred_menopausal_state,
    pr_status,
    surgery = type_of_breast_surgery,
    radio_therapy,

    tsc2,
    bbc3,
    sbno1,
    ptprd_mut,
    nek1_mut,
    mapk14,
    kmt2c
  )

hx_dat2 <- hx_dat1 %>%
  mutate(
    across(where(is.character), ~ na_if(.x, "")),

    er_status       = as.character(er_status),
    hormone_therapy = as.character(hormone_therapy),
    chemotherapy    = as.character(chemotherapy),

    grade            = as.character(grade),
    her2             = as.character(her2),
    pam50            = as.character(pam50),
    tumor_stage      = as.character(tumor_stage),
    menopausal_state = as.character(menopausal_state),
    pr_status        = as.character(pr_status),
    surgery          = as.character(surgery),
    radio_therapy    = as.character(radio_therapy),

    os_months      = as.numeric(os_months),
    os_event       = as.numeric(os_event),
    age            = as.numeric(age),
    tumor_size     = as.numeric(tumor_size),
    nodes_positive = as.numeric(nodes_positive),

    tsc2   = as.numeric(tsc2),
    bbc3   = as.numeric(bbc3),
    sbno1  = as.numeric(sbno1),
    mapk14 = as.numeric(mapk14),
    kmt2c  = as.numeric(kmt2c)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    hormone_therapy_num = case_when(
      hormone_therapy %in% c("1", 1) ~ 1,
      hormone_therapy %in% c("0", 0) ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    chemotherapy_num = case_when(
      chemotherapy %in% c("1", 1) ~ 1,
      chemotherapy %in% c("0", 0) ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    ptprd_mut_bin = case_when(
      is.na(ptprd_mut) ~ NA_real_,
      ptprd_mut %in% c("0", 0) ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ 1
    ),
    nek1_mut_bin = case_when(
      is.na(nek1_mut) ~ NA_real_,
      nek1_mut %in% c("0", 0) ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ 1
    )
  )

hx_dat_er <- hx_dat2 %>%
  filter(
    er_status == "Positive",
    hormone_therapy_num == 1
  )
# set up data for crude cox and aft
hx_dat_model <- hx_dat_er %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(os_months),
    !is.na(os_event),
    !is.na(chemotherapy_num),
    !is.na(age),
    !is.na(tumor_size),
    !is.na(nodes_positive),
    !is.na(grade),
    !is.na(pam50),
    !is.na(tsc2),
    !is.na(bbc3),
    !is.na(sbno1),
    !is.na(ptprd_mut_bin),
    !is.na(nek1_mut_bin)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    chemo = factor(
      chemotherapy_num,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("Hormone only", "Hormone + Chemo")
    ),
    chemo_num = chemotherapy_num,
    grade = factor(grade),
    pam50_grp = case_when(
      pam50 %in% c("LumA", "LumB") ~ pam50,
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pam50_grp = factor(pam50_grp, levels = c("LumA", "LumB", "Other")),
    ptprd_mut_bin = factor(ptprd_mut_bin, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    nek1_mut_bin  = factor(nek1_mut_bin,  levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes"))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(pam50_grp))

nrow(hx_dat_model)
[1] 1000
table(hx_dat_model$chemo, useNA = "ifany")

   Hormone only Hormone + Chemo 
            880             120 
table(hx_dat_model$grade, useNA = "ifany")

  1   2   3 
101 458 441 
table(hx_dat_model$pam50_grp, useNA = "ifany")

 LumA  LumB Other 
  440   355   205

6.2 Cox model without time-dependent chemotherapy

# fit crude cox model
hx_cox_formula <- Surv(os_months, os_event) ~
  chemo +
  age +
  tumor_size +
  nodes_positive +
  grade +
  pam50_grp +
  tsc2 +
  bbc3 +
  sbno1 +
  ptprd_mut_bin +
  nek1_mut_bin

hx_cox_fit <- coxph(
  hx_cox_formula,
  data = hx_dat_model,
  ties = "breslow"
)

summary(hx_cox_fit)
Call:
coxph(formula = hx_cox_formula, data = hx_dat_model, ties = "breslow")

  n= 1000, number of events= 422 

                          coef exp(coef)  se(coef)      z Pr(>|z|)

nodes_positive       -0.015179  0.984936  0.019425 -0.781  0.43456    
grade2               -0.067654  0.934584  0.150925 -0.448  0.65396    
grade3               -0.333260  0.716584  0.160837 -2.072  0.03826 *  
pam50_grpLumB         0.013184  1.013271  0.129372  0.102  0.91883    
pam50_grpOther        0.007818  1.007849  0.130306  0.060  0.95216    
tsc2                 -0.359188  0.698243  0.056222 -6.389 1.67e-10 ***

sbno1                -0.182353  0.833307  0.058663 -3.108  0.00188 ** 
ptprd_mut_binYes     -0.781777  0.457592  0.454406 -1.720  0.08535 .  
nek1_mut_binYes      -0.984378  0.373672  0.509280 -1.933  0.05325 .

                     exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95

Concordance= 0.67  (se = 0.014 )

Wald test            = 142.5  on 13 df,   p=<2e-16
Score (logrank) test = 149.7  on 13 df,   p=<2e-16
# vif check
hx_lm_proxy <- lm(
  os_months ~ chemo + age + tumor_size + nodes_positive + grade +
    pam50_grp + tsc2 + bbc3 + sbno1 + ptprd_mut_bin + nek1_mut_bin,
  data = hx_dat_model
)

vif(hx_lm_proxy)
                   GVIF Df GVIF^(1/(2*Df))

nek1_mut_bin   1.016419  1        1.008176
# ph assumption check
hx_ph_res <- cox.zph(hx_cox_fit)
hx_ph_res
                 chisq df       p
chemo          24.6473  1 6.9e-07
age             0.0931  1 0.76027
tumor_size      1.9145  1 0.16647
nodes_positive  1.3864  1 0.23902

pam50_grp       2.6564  2 0.26496
tsc2            0.2480  1 0.61848

nek1_mut_bin    0.4913  1 0.48335
GLOBAL         38.5017 13 0.00024
plot(hx_ph_res)

6.3 Cox model with time-dependent chemotherapy

# time-dependent cox
hx_event_times <- sort(unique(hx_dat_model$os_months[hx_dat_model$os_event == 1]))
hx_event_times <- hx_event_times[-1]

hx_dat_split <- survSplit(
  data  = hx_dat_model,
  cut   = hx_event_times,
  end   = "os_months",
  start = "t0",
  event = "os_event"
) %>%
  mutate(
    chemo_num = ifelse(chemo == "Hormone + Chemo", 1, 0),
    chemo_t   = chemo_num * log(os_months)
  )
hx_cox_td_fit <- coxph(
  Surv(t0, os_months, os_event) ~
    chemo_num +
    chemo_t +
    age +
    tumor_size +
    nodes_positive +
    grade +
    pam50_grp +
    tsc2 +
    bbc3 +
    sbno1 +
    ptprd_mut_bin +
    nek1_mut_bin,
  data = hx_dat_split,
  ties = "breslow"
)

summary(hx_cox_td_fit)
Call:
coxph(formula = Surv(t0, os_months, os_event) ~ chemo_num + chemo_t + 
    age + tumor_size + nodes_positive + grade + pam50_grp + tsc2 + 
    bbc3 + sbno1 + ptprd_mut_bin + nek1_mut_bin, data = hx_dat_split, 
    ties = "breslow")

  n= 162869, number of events= 422 

                      coef exp(coef)  se(coef)      z Pr(>|z|)    
chemo_num        -5.753353  0.003172  1.873552 -3.071 0.002135 ** 
chemo_t           1.425863  4.161448  0.394054  3.618 0.000296 ***
age              -0.004696  0.995315  0.005057 -0.929 0.353055    
tumor_size       -0.005671  0.994345  0.004639 -1.222 0.221565    
nodes_positive   -0.013999  0.986099  0.019633 -0.713 0.475824    
grade2           -0.084470  0.918999  0.151083 -0.559 0.576097    
grade3           -0.350385  0.704417  0.161113 -2.175 0.029647 *  
pam50_grpLumB     0.028416  1.028824  0.129063  0.220 0.825735

ptprd_mut_binYes -0.849538  0.427612  0.455046 -1.867 0.061912 .  
nek1_mut_binYes  -1.033116  0.355896  0.509255 -2.029 0.042491 *

                 exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95

chemo_t           4.161448     0.2403 1.922e+00    9.0087
age               0.995315     1.0047 9.855e-01    1.0052
tumor_size        0.994345     1.0057 9.853e-01    1.0034

Concordance= 0.676  (se = 0.016 )

Score (logrank) test = 173.4  on 14 df,   p=<2e-16
# ph check for td cox
hx_ph_td_res <- cox.zph(hx_cox_td_fit)
hx_ph_td_res
                 chisq df     p
chemo_num       5.1237  1 0.024
chemo_t         5.0270  1 0.025
age             0.5148  1 0.473
tumor_size      0.8472  1 0.357
nodes_positive  0.2160  1 0.642
grade           5.3681  2 0.068
pam50_grp       2.9096  2 0.233
tsc2            0.7018  1 0.402
bbc3            0.0973  1 0.755
sbno1           4.1144  1 0.043
ptprd_mut_bin   0.5924  1 0.441
nek1_mut_bin    0.4908  1 0.484
GLOBAL         18.7606 14 0.174
plot(hx_ph_td_res)

6.4 Anderson’s plot

# draw anderson plot
hx_reptime <- function(l, t, range_of_time) {
  x <- numeric(length(range_of_time))
  for (i in seq_along(range_of_time)) {
    dif <- range_of_time[i] - t
    dif <- dif[dif >= 0]
    if (length(dif) > 0) {
      x[i] <- l[which.min(dif)]
    } else {
      x[i] <- 0
    }
  }
  x
}

hx_dat_anderson <- hx_dat_model %>%
  mutate(chemo_num = ifelse(chemo == "Hormone + Chemo", 1, 0))

hx_fit_and <- basehaz(
  coxph(
    Surv(os_months, os_event) ~ strata(chemo_num),
    data = hx_dat_anderson,
    ties = "breslow"
  ),
  centered = FALSE
)

hx_H0 <- hx_fit_and$hazard[hx_fit_and$strata == "chemo_num=0"]
hx_H1 <- hx_fit_and$hazard[hx_fit_and$strata == "chemo_num=1"]

hx_t0 <- hx_fit_and$time[hx_fit_and$strata == "chemo_num=0"]
hx_t1 <- hx_fit_and$time[hx_fit_and$strata == "chemo_num=1"]

hx_grid_time <- sort(unique(c(hx_t0, hx_t1)))

hx_H0_rep <- hx_reptime(hx_H0, hx_t0, hx_grid_time)
hx_H1_rep <- hx_reptime(hx_H1, hx_t1, hx_grid_time)

plot(
  hx_H1_rep ~ hx_H0_rep,
  main = "Anderson Plot for Chemotherapy Groups",
  xlab = "Cumulative Hazard: Hormone only",
  ylab = "Cumulative Hazard: Hormone + Chemo",
  type = "s"
)
abline(0, 1, lty = 2, col = "red")

6.5 Cox-snell residual plot

# draw cox-snell residual plot
hx_cs_resid <- predict(hx_cox_td_fit, type = "expected")

hx_fit_cs <- survfit(Surv(hx_cs_resid, os_event) ~ 1, data = hx_dat_split)

hx_H_cs <- cumsum(hx_fit_cs$n.event / hx_fit_cs$n.risk)

plot(
  hx_fit_cs$time, hx_H_cs,
  type = "s",
  col = "blue",
  main = "Cox-Snell Residual Plot\nFinal Time-Dependent Cox Model",
  xlab = "Cox-Snell Residual",
  ylab = "Nelson-Aalen Cumulative Hazard"
)
abline(0, 1, col = "red", lty = 2)

7 AFT models

# fit aft models
hx_aft_formula <- Surv(os_months, os_event) ~
  chemo +
  age +
  tumor_size +
  nodes_positive +
  grade +
  pam50_grp +
  tsc2 +
  bbc3 +
  sbno1 +
  ptprd_mut_bin +
  nek1_mut_bin

hx_fit_exp <- survreg(hx_aft_formula, data = hx_dat_model, dist = "exponential")
hx_fit_weib <- survreg(hx_aft_formula, data = hx_dat_model, dist = "weibull")
hx_fit_lnorm <- survreg(hx_aft_formula, data = hx_dat_model, dist = "lognormal")
hx_fit_llogis <- survreg(hx_aft_formula, data = hx_dat_model, dist = "loglogistic")
# compare aft models
hx_model_compare <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Exponential", "Weibull", "Log-normal", "Log-logistic"),
  AIC = c(AIC(hx_fit_exp), AIC(hx_fit_weib), AIC(hx_fit_lnorm), AIC(hx_fit_llogis)),
  BIC = c(BIC(hx_fit_exp), BIC(hx_fit_weib), BIC(hx_fit_lnorm), BIC(hx_fit_llogis)),
  logLik = c(logLik(hx_fit_exp), logLik(hx_fit_weib), logLik(hx_fit_lnorm), logLik(hx_fit_llogis))
) %>%
  arrange(AIC)

hx_model_compare
         Model      AIC      BIC    logLik

n= 1000 
# extract aft time ratios
hx_extract_aft_table <- function(fit) {
  tb <- as.data.frame(summary(fit)$table)
  tb$term <- rownames(tb)

  tb %>%
    mutate(
      lower = Value - 1.96 * `Std. Error`,
      upper = Value + 1.96 * `Std. Error`,
      TR = exp(Value),
      TR_lower = exp(lower),
      TR_upper = exp(upper)
    ) %>%
    select(term, Value, `Std. Error`, p, TR, TR_lower, TR_upper)
}

hx_aft_exp_table <- hx_extract_aft_table(hx_fit_exp)
hx_aft_weib_table <- hx_extract_aft_table(hx_fit_weib)
hx_aft_lnorm_table <- hx_extract_aft_table(hx_fit_lnorm)
hx_aft_llogis_table <- hx_extract_aft_table(hx_fit_llogis)

hx_aft_weib_table

# plot adjusted survival from weibull aft

library(flexsurv)
Warning: package 'flexsurv' was built under R version 4.4.3
hx_fit_weib_flex <- flexsurvreg(
  hx_aft_formula,
  data = hx_dat_model,
  dist = "weibull"
)

hx_newdata_typical <- data.frame(
  chemo = factor(c("Hormone only", "Hormone + Chemo"),
                 levels = levels(hx_dat_model$chemo)),
  age = mean(hx_dat_model$age, na.rm = TRUE),
  tumor_size = mean(hx_dat_model$tumor_size, na.rm = TRUE),
  nodes_positive = mean(hx_dat_model$nodes_positive, na.rm = TRUE),
  grade = factor(levels(hx_dat_model$grade)[1], levels = levels(hx_dat_model$grade)),
  pam50_grp = factor("LumA", levels = levels(hx_dat_model$pam50_grp)),
  tsc2 = mean(hx_dat_model$tsc2, na.rm = TRUE),
  bbc3 = mean(hx_dat_model$bbc3, na.rm = TRUE),
  sbno1 = mean(hx_dat_model$sbno1, na.rm = TRUE),
  ptprd_mut_bin = factor("No", levels = levels(hx_dat_model$ptprd_mut_bin)),
  nek1_mut_bin = factor("No", levels = levels(hx_dat_model$nek1_mut_bin))
)

hx_pred <- summary(
  hx_fit_weib_flex,
  newdata = hx_newdata_typical,
  type = "survival",
  t = seq(0, 250, by = 1)
)

plot(
  hx_pred[[1]]$time, hx_pred[[1]]$est,
  type = "l", lwd = 2,
  xlab = "Time since diagnosis (months)",
  ylab = "Adjusted survival probability",
  main = "Adjusted Survival Curves from Weibull AFT"
)

lines(hx_pred[[2]]$time, hx_pred[[2]]$est, lwd = 2, lty = 2)

legend(
  "topright",
  legend = c("Hormone only", "Hormone + Chemo"),
  lwd = 2,
  lty = c(1, 2)
)

8 Model Comparisons

8.1 c-index

# clinical-only time-dependent cox
ccox_td_score <- -predict(ccox_td_fit, type = "lp")
ccox_td_c <- concordance(
  Surv(t0, os_months, os_event) ~ ccox_td_score,
  data = dat_ccox_split
)

# weighted time-dependent cox
cox_w_td_score <- -predict(cox_w_td, type = "lp")
cox_w_td_c <- concordance(
  Surv(t0, os_months, os_event) ~ cox_w_td_score,
  data = dat_split_w
)

# crude cox + gene time-dependent
hx_cox_td_score <- -predict(hx_cox_td_fit, type = "lp")
hx_cox_td_c <- concordance(
  Surv(t0, os_months, os_event) ~ hx_cox_td_score,
  data = hx_dat_split
)

# aft (weibull)
weib_score <- predict(hx_fit_weib, type = "lp")
weib_c <- concordance(
  Surv(os_months, os_event) ~ weib_score,
  data = hx_dat_model
)

# combine results
cindex_compare <- data.frame(
  model = c(
    "Clinical-only TD Cox",
    "Weighted TD Cox",
    "TD Cox + Gene",
    "AFT Weibull"
  ),
  c_index = c(
    ccox_td_c$concordance,
    cox_w_td_c$concordance,
    hx_cox_td_c$concordance,
    weib_c$concordance
  ),
  se = c(
    sqrt(ccox_td_c$var),
    sqrt(cox_w_td_c$var),
    sqrt(hx_cox_td_c$var),
    sqrt(weib_c$var)
  )
)

cindex_compare
                 model   c_index         se
1 Clinical-only TD Cox 0.6127093 0.01719844
2      Weighted TD Cox 0.6068989 0.01686557
3        TD Cox + Gene 0.6762854 0.01559798
4          AFT Weibull 0.6708960 0.01444206

The concordance indices indicate moderate discriminative performance across the survival models. The clinical-only time-dependent Cox model achieved a C-index of 0.613, while the IPTW weighted time-dependent Cox model produced a very similar value of 0.607, suggesting that weighting to address confounding by indication had little impact on the model’s overall predictive discrimination. In contrast, incorporating the selected gene variables substantially improved model performance. The time-dependent Cox model with gene predictors achieved a higher C-index of 0.676, indicating better ability to distinguish patients with different survival outcomes. The Weibull AFT model showed comparable discrimination with a C-index of 0.671, suggesting that the parametric AFT framework provides similar predictive capability to the Cox model when using the same set of predictors. Overall, these results suggest that while propensity score weighting primarily adjusts treatment effect estimation, the inclusion of selected gene variables meaningfully improves the model’s ability to predict survival.
